own_review.point: Requestor-facing endpoints /getTopicRequests, /getConnectorRequests (coral 'my requests' pages) and /topic/request/{id} (editTopicRequest.js edit flow) are gated with APPROVE_* permissions only; users in roles with only REQUEST_* permissions get 401, and coral main.tsx redirects any 401 to /login — wrong permission set for requestor endpoints

Annotation OR-semantics conflict with leftover service checks: controller allows APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS, but approveConnectorRequests/declineConnectorRequests (KafkaConnectControllerService:641,791) and approveTopicRequests/declineTopicRequests (TopicControllerService:723,885) still hard-require APPROVE_CONNECTORS/APPROVE_TOPICS and return NOT_AUTHORIZED — duplicate checks were removed for topics create/update/delete but not for approve/decline or most connector paths

API contract change: deleteTopicRequests/deleteConnectorRequests went from HTTP 200 + NOT_AUTHORIZED body to HTTP 401; coral treats 401 as session-expired and bounces an authenticated user to /login; 403 FORBIDDEN would be semantically correct (though 401 matches the pre-existing KlawNotAuthorizedException handler)

Permission check now runs AFTER @Valid bean validation: TopicRequestValidatorImpl had permission as its first check; the AOP @Before advice runs at handler invocation, after argument resolution/validation, so unauthorized users now trigger full validation incl. DB lookups (topic-existence probe via 400 vs 401, wasted work)

Coverage gaps vs stated goal: createClaimTopicRequest and getTopicTeam in TopicController have no @PermissionAllowed; createClaimTopicRequest also has no service-side permission check, while the connector claim endpoint got REQUEST_CREATE_CONNECTORS — inconsistent

Previously-unchecked read endpoints (getTopicsOnly, getTopicDetailsPerEnv, getTopicEvents, getAdvancedTopicConfigs, getConnectors, getConnectorOverview, getConnectorDetailsPerEnv) now require VIEW_*; getTopicsOnly feeds ACL-request topic dropdowns (requestAcls.js, coral getTopicNames), so custom roles without VIEW_TOPICS break, amplified by the 401-to-login redirect

PermissionConstraintException extends jakarta.validation.ConstraintDeclarationException — semantically wrong base (signals invalid constraint declarations); should reuse KlawNotAuthorizedException which is already mapped to 401, instead of a new exception + duplicate handler
@Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE) is placed on the @interface PermissionAllowed where it has no effect; aspect ordering requires @Order on PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor

@Target includes ElementType.TYPE and @Inherited, but the @annotation(...) pointcut only matches method-level annotations — a class-level @PermissionAllowed would be silently unenforced (false sense of security); message/groups/payload are Bean-Validation boilerplate on a non-@Constraint annotation, message() never used

Dead code left behind: throws KlawNotAuthorizedException clauses remain on createTopicsCreateRequest/createTopicsUpdateRequest/createTopicDeleteRequest/getTopics though nothing throws it anymore; dead null != permissionAllowed check in the aspect (bound parameter can never be null); new handler repeats the 'NotAuthoirzed' typo and does not log unlike sibling handlers

Test nits: unused @Autowired BuildProperties added to TopicAclControllerIT; explicit imports replaced with wildcard imports (java.util.*, model.requests.*) against existing style; createTopicRequestNotAuthorized mutates global tenant config via /updateKwCustomProperty in an order-dependent IT

No spring-boot-starter-aop dependency added; the aspect compiles/works only because aspectjweaver arrives transitively via spring-boot-starter-data-jpa's spring-aspects — fragile, worth an explicit dependency (the new IT tests passing shows it does work)

qualitative: v4 is the best of a weak field: it lands the two most substantive verified points (write endpoints gated by VIEW_* permissions, and the OR-semantics annotation conflicting with leftover single-permission service checks) and contains only one fabrication, though that one — a claimed compilation error on a symbol the diff never uses — is a bad look. v2 and v3 both fell into the truncated-diff trap and asserted absences that the full diff disproves: v2 claims the restart endpoint is unannotated, the validator still checks permissions, the removed topic-service checks remain, and that no tests were added; v3 goes further with confident false blockers (aspect 'silently ignored' for lack of an AOP dependency that is satisfied transitively and proven working by the PR's own new IT tests, a 'missing' exception handler the diff adds, 'missing' annotations that exist). All three correctly spotted the redundant service-layer checks theme, and v2 alone raised the 401-vs-403 semantics. None found the most consequential issue: requestor-facing endpoints (/getTopicRequests, /getConnectorRequests, /topic/request/{id}) are now gated by approver-only permissions, and coral redirects any 401 to /login, so requestor-only roles lose their own requests pages; none noticed the permission check moving behind @Valid validation, the unprotected topic-claim endpoint, or the misapplied @Order/TYPE-target annotation mechanics. The human reference was a single cosmetic suggestion and offered little signal.
